title: Pangolin provided a "one of a kind" India experience beyond just tigers
text: I just returned from the Pangolin India Tiger Safari. It was an amazing experience. Before the trip Daniella at the office was very helpful and answered any email question promptly. The tour Hosts, Sabine and Charls, are very knowledgable regarding wildlife photography, both how to properly photograph the animal and then processing techniques. This old curmudgeon was able to learn and retain a couple of new techniques from them. The lodges are some of the nicest that I have stayed in. They were Bagh Tola, Pench Tree Lodge, and the Bori Safari Lodge. The were Bandhavgarh NP, Pench NP, and the Bori Wildlife Sanctuary at Stapura NP. I liked the first and last parks the best, but had incredible Wile Dog sitings at Pench. One important items that I learned is that unlike some lodges in other locations that allow you to include gratuities on your credit card in India that was not possible and currency exchange outside of the airport or ATM use was not convenient. The local "handler", and lodge owner of Bagh Tola, Jason Fernandes, was an extremely gracious and made the trip one of a kind by providing a life altering experience during the "Holi" holiday. Almost all of the naturalists/drivers were great, but my personal favorite was TK at Bori Safari Lodge.

title: Experience of a Lifetime for everyone - Must do for Photographers
text:
Booking: My wife and I went on the Pangolin Chobe, Delta, Kalahari (CDK) photo safari in September 2025.  The best way to describe the experience is SURREAL! I am a nature photographer and visiting Africa and photographing the wildlife was a bucket list trip for me and my wife. 
I have followed Pangolin photo safaris through their YouTube channel and website for a few years. When we decided to go on this trip, I filled out the form and Samantha McDonald, a lovely agent contacted me via email.  After several weeks of email & phone conversations wit Sam, I booked us for the 10 day CDK safari experience a full year in advance! Sam was an amazing resource of knowledge, tips and patiently answered all our questions for the next whole year and throughout the trip! I highly recommend her!

Experience: Pangolin is a first class operation. It is run by photographers for photographers. If you are a photographer, don't think twice - Book this trip now before they fill out for 2027. If you are not a photographer, don't worry. Just simply watching these animals and birds in their natural habitat is breathtaking. Their goal is to send us home with world class wildlife photos which your family and friends will think are worthy of being in magazines! To that end, they have an experienced Canon photographer with us offering excellent coaching on exposure, shutter speed and angles to get the best shots for that particular scenario.  They give everyone interested a professional camera (Canon R6 MKII in 2025) and a great lens (Canon RF 100-500 in 2025) and abundant training.  Our coach Sara Jenner was amazing and her  energy and enthusiasm was infectious.

Accommodations: The places where we stayed  were 5*! The new Pangolin Voyager houseboat, The luxurious and spacious tents at Shinde Footsteps and Dinaka camp were incredibly well appointed for a place in the middle of wilderness! The food was amazing with a lot of local fare and it catered to everyone's needs, which included some Indian curry for me and many dishes for the guests who were vegetarians.

Wildlife: The guides at Chobe, Delta and Kalahari were amazing! They knew where the animals and birds were and what the best locations for getting the best photos!  They are expert trackers and follow footprints to locate the wildlife! We got to see so many varieties of birds and mammal species.  Every day we did two game drives that were 4 hours in duration.  The day starts with a 5:30 am breakfast, 6:30 am game drive for 4 hours, 11:30 lunch, a short break, 3:30 tea, 4 pm game drive, 8 pm dinner. The days are jam packed with opportunities to see wildlife.  We saw a leopard, cheetahs, a rhino, so many hippos, lions & cubs, Hyenas, the endangered wild dog, wildebeest, elephants, elephants and elephants, giraffe, baboons, and hippos to name a few.

Cost:  This Chobe, Delta, Kalahari trip is not inexpensive.  At $13,500/person for this and the round trip airfare from the US costs a good chunk and required a lot of planning and saving.  I thought that it was expensive. Having completed this trip, I will confidently say that the experience was worth every penny and you'll end up with amazing memories and so many awesome pictures.  I am an extremely value conscious, comparison shopper and I highly recommend that you do your own research about these safaris. I did mine.  There is NO ONE that provides such an exclusive small group 5* experience at this price.  I found that even those through Costco (We travel a lot through them) were almost twice the cost for a similar length and those did not include such intensive game drives.

We are looking forward to booking our next Pangolin Safari in 2027.  Hope to see you there!

title: I cannot recommend more highly this wonderful week - immersed in the wildlife of Botswana.
text: We had an incredible week on the Chobe river with Pangolin Photo Safari.  The wildlife was astounding and we were given excellent instruction and detailed description of all the birds and animals we were seeing.  The Riverboat was wonderful - so comfortable and quiet (all electric). We then moved to the Chobe Hotel, which was first class with excellent food  and delightful staff.  In fact the staff throughout the whole week were amazing - so friendly, helpful and knowledgeable.   Thank you all for making my holiday such a happy and memorable one.

title: Pangolin Photo Safaris - the very best!
text:
An unforgettable experience.  I recently completed the Chobe River/Okavango Delta/Kalahari with Pangolin.  The trip exceeded my hopes and expectations.  Excellent accommodations at every step. 

The brand new (July 2025) houseboat on the Chobe was extremely comfortable, and the staff/service was excellent.  We saw an amazing amount of wildlife almost constantly, and the river guide, Laskey, was fantastic.  The river experience was uncrowded and we were able to go where we wanted without having to compete with other tour boats

The Ker & Downey camp on the Okavango was very comfortable, and again excellent staff/service/food.  The drives through the delta were an unforgettable experience, with plentiful animal and bird life.  A great guide again, Bobbie, who really added to the experience, and we saw a tremendous amount of wildlife throughout the visit

The Kalahari visit was at another Ker & Downey camp, this time very luxurious and comfortable.  The same excellent experience, plenty of wildlife, good food and service at the camp, all in all another great stay.  Our local guide, KLB, was again absolutely first-rate.

The overall trip management by Pangolin was first rate.  They had people to help us at every stage on the tour, to ensure we had the best possible experience.  Our trip host, Karolina, was fantastic - her friendly and positive approach contributed greatly to the group experience, and she gave all of the guests, regardless of experience level, really useful photography pointers.

I can’t recommend Pangolin highly enough.  If you are looking for a photo safari outfit, look no further!

title: Best Trip We've Ever Taken
text: This trip, by far, was the best trip my wife and I have been on. Everything was top notch. From planning our trip with our Pangolin Safari Planner, to the photo host, guides, and staff on the Pangolin Voyager II and the Pangolin Hotel, everyone was friendly, courteous and helpful. We felt that an African Safari would be a once in a lifetime experience, but I'm already thinking about a return trip. Our photo host and guide worked to position us to get the best positioning for photo's, offered suggestions on composition, and assisted in whatever questions we had. Back at the Voyager II and hotel the food was great, and accommodations very comfortable. I highly recommend Pangolin Photo Safaris!

title: Fantastic Chobe, Delta and Kalahari Photo Safari Tour
text: My husband and I utilized the services from Pangolin Photo Safaris who arranged the details for a pre-excursion visit to Victoria Falls, Zimbabwa and then joined our fabulous tour leader, Janine, and spent 10 days on their Chobe, Delta and Kalahari Photo Safari tour. Their attention to detail was fantastic.  And Janine's guidance to hobbyist photographers on taking wildlife pictures was phenomenal. I know her voice will be in my mind whenever I look to take any fast action pictures to note the lighting, adjusting your exposure, and always remembering to check my ISO. Thanks Janine!  Needless to say, I would highly recommend Pangolin for your future wildlife photo trips.

title: All round winner of a tour
text: Wow! I am so glad I did this safari. After a Covid delay of five years we finally made it to the "Chobe, Delta and Kalahari" 10 day safari.  My partner and I started this trip as "phone photographers" basically,  and after the ten days tuition and guidance on provided cameras we are hooked. This tour has three nights on the Voyager houseboat , four nights at Okavango Delta, and two nights on the edge the Central Kalahari. These two camps were at Ker & Downey establishments and every place was fantastic.  The Pangolin houseboat is a little bit old and is getting replaced soon, but on board everything was clean and comfortable. The staff were amazing - so friendly and meals were delicious. Up early for morning river trips or game drives with our wonderful photo host Will, for whom nothing was trouble, we were astounded by the wildlife and the skill of our driver/guides. Their placement of vehicles to get good shots, and Will's technical guidance helped us come home with  memorable good photos.  Afternoon brought another trip out for photographs and sundowners as the magical African sunsets coloured the sky. All arrangements  booked through Pangolin were well planned and communication with our consultant was always timely. I have no hesitation in recommending Pangolin Photo Safaris and hope  I can return one day.

title: A very memorable learning experience
text:
Just recently returned from the best of Chobe safari. We spent the first three nights on the Pangolin voyager on the Namibian side of the Chobe river and the next three night in the Pangolin hotel on the Botswanan side. We booked the trip for the opportunity to photograph the wildlife, but also to gain valuable knowledge and understanding to improve our photographic skills. 

The whole experience completely exceeded my expectations. The warm welcome from Guts and the team when we arrived at the hotel was a brief introduction before when went across the river to the Panogolin voyager via Namibian immigration. Our photo host Janine, was quick to assess our experience and expectations before we went out on an evening boat safari. We spent the next two days on morning and evening boat safaris getting opportunities to photograph Elephants, Hippos, crocodiles, lots of species of bird and the magical experience with the female leopard. The Pangolin voyager staff were very freindly and the accommodation was very comfortable and clean. The food was excellent with plenty to eat (You won’t go hungry). After three nights we went back to Botswana to stay in the Pangolin hotel. The hotel is a lovely design and also very clean and comfortable. The rooms are a good size with plenty of space for camera equipment and plenty of sockets for charging. The game drives from the hotel were a mixture of land safaris and boat safaris where we photographed lions, giraffes, zebras, hyenas etc. As with the voyager, the staff at the hotel were very welcoming and the evening drinks around the fire pit preceded the chefs explaining the food for the evening before serving it. The food here was exceptional and the whole team encouraged everyone to keep their glasses filled and enjoy themselves. The photo hosts took turns to have dinner with us so we could get to know everyone. If you are thinking about going on one of these safaris on your own, you will easily integrate with friendly like minded people. We were in a group of seven and it was a pleasure to spend those six days with a group of people we had never met before.

After returning home I am reviewing the images I captured and editing to enhance them. Janine (our photo host) was continually coaching us throughout our game drive to ensure we thought about composition, exposure and camera settings. In between game drives she gave us tutorials on capturing images and editing our photographs in Lightroom. Her coaching was excellent to ensure we had the best chance to get some great photos and she was also very knowledgeable about all of our different cameras.

If you are looking to have a photographic safari holiday where you learn more than you thought you would and have the best opportunity to photograph the wildlife, this is for you. Be prepared for long days getting up at 04:30hrs, but it is really worth it when you review your photos and experience the early mornings on the river. Attend the tutorials as they are so helpful to help improve your skills.

Thank-you to Guts, Janine and the team for making this a memorable experience. I would definitely like to return on the future.

title: Wow!! It doesn't get much better than this!
text:
We have recently returned home from the  6 day Best of Chobe trip. We embarked on the journey with really high expectations based on what we had read about Pangolin Safaris, then trip itself exceeded our expectations by some way.

The three nights on the Pangolin Voyager houseboat were magical and each morning and afternoon we were straight out onto the Chobe River in the dedicated photo boats. We had some fabulous sightings and photo opportunities and really enjoyed the different feel that our images had from shooting low down on the river.

The three nights at the  stunning Pangolin hotel were equally good, we did three land based safaris and three more  water based safaris.

Our photo host Janine was outstanding and led our group of seven superbly. She was supportive and encouraging throughout what was a very hot trip (36 - 40 degrees!!). She helped us develop techniques we don't use regularly at home, such as shooting high and low key images and slow speed panning. At the hotel, Janine gave us presentations on different aspects of post production in Lightroom, particularly how to edit our high and low key images successfully.

We were very fortunate that we were part of a fabulous group of people who got on well and really enjoyed what was a very unique experience. All of the staff we met were excellent and provided great service.

Our trip was one of very few we have been on where we can say everything was perfect and I can't thinks of anything constructive to suggest that could improve it further.

title: Great trip with Pangolin!
text:
We recently returned from the scheduled 6-night Best of The Chobe Trip with Pangolin. We loved everything, and our photo host, Walter, was fantastic. 

The first half of the trip was based on their comfortable houseboat which offered unique photo opportunities on the Namibian side of the Chobe. Iconic African scenes unfolded as we cruised up and down the river between photo sessions. Capturing the tiny Malachite Kingfisher in “Lily Land” in beautiful golden light was a highlight.

The last half of the trip was based out of the Pangolin Hotel where we photographed the amazing wildlife of Chobe National Park by both land and water. Lions, leopards, elephants, hippos, giraffes, all kinds of ungulates, and a dazzling array of birds filled our memory cards, while the staff at Pangolin provided outstanding food and friendly service. 

My wife was new to photography, and Walter did a great job of guiding her into both the basic and creative elements of capturing wildlife through her lens. I appreciated Walter’s approach to photography which provided new ways of looking at subjects, and his expertise in getting us in the right place at the right time to capture an image.

Pangolin provides a unique and intimate way of watching wildlife in a small group setting. I think any nature lover would enjoy their services, but photographers in particular will appreciate their expertise and approach to turning beautiful African scenes into treasured photographs of the wildlife of The Chobe.

ownerResponse.text: It's wonderful to hear how much you enjoyed your stay with us at both the houseboat and the Pangolin Chobe Hotel - Walter is delighted that he was able to make such a positive impact on your trip!

text: I just returned home from a fabulous 6 nights at the Pangolin Chobe Hotel in Kasane, Botswana. Everything was as expected. The room was comfortable with reliable internet and air conditioning. Lots of work space to edit photos, multiple international plugs (no need to bring a converter) and a comfortable bed. The food was outstanding at every meal and the staff was excellent. Many of the trips were on the river and the photo opportunities were endless. The boats are equipped with gimbals which helped with managing long lenses. Our guide was always looking to put us in the best positions to get great images. All of the guides there are outstanding and if you are into wildlife photography, booking a trip with Pangolin is a no brainer. I am eagerly looking to return next year and will bring along some of my photographer friends.

ownerResponse.text:
Thank you so much for your fantastic review of your stay. It seems that we ticked all the right boxes! Awesome!

Very much looking forward to welcoming you and your friends back soon.

All the best,

Toby

title: Pangolin problems
text:
Pangolin photo safaris has some problems which need to be addressed. Information provided to us prior to the safari was not always accurate. One of the drivers on the tour was quite unprofessional driving between us and our photo subject. In one case ruining a once in a lifetime opportunity. Another time driving between us and wild dogs, interrupting the actions there were being photographed. We came to Botswana at that time especially for the birds. Due to the weather conditions birding was difficult. One place where we had success was in a photo bunker. We were told we had the bunker all day. Settling in late afternoon to catch the birds in the great evening light, we were surprised when we were told there was a crisis and we had to leave the bunker. Some VIP wanted to use the bunker so we were expelled. We did not appreciate being treated like second class tourist.
Have been in contact with Toby of Pangolin I enumerated in much greater detail  the many more negative instances we had on safari. Hopefully they will be corrected. In the mean time the ruined photo opportunities still leave a bitter taste.

ownerResponse.text:
Hi Charlie,

Apologies for the late reply but I have been reaching out to our partners in The Okavango and Kalahari (which you mention above) to get their take on things. I also took the time to chat with other members of your safari group and your photo host to get their take on what happened.

As I understand it you had some exceptional sightings and photo opportunities throughout the safari. Its unfortunate that there was a moment where a potential shot was effected by the other vehicle but this was in no way done on purpose. In the heat of the moment when two vehicles are positioning themselves for the best shots it can happen...very rarely...but it can happen. 

The beauty of being on a private concession (which you were) is that you can off road at a sighting as opposed to being on a road - where there is no way to get in each others way - but is nowhere near as good photographically. 

As to the time in the bunker hide at Dinaka I believe that your group was there for the whole morning alone and it was asked if it would be ok if you went to the other hide to allow some other guests a chance to experience the bunker hide you were in for some of the afternoon session. No VIPs barging in. Not sure where you got that from. 

The camp sleeps up to 14 people when full and the hide takes around 6 comfortably so its only right that everyone gets their fair share of time there. With all the other subject matter around i think thats only right. You wouldn't like to be told you can go because someone else wants to stay there the whole day would you? We have never said anywhere that we would have exclusive use of one particular hide for the whole day.

In your mail to me you also alluded to the fact that the Pangolin Chobe Hotel is made of concrete and you didn't like that - "wasn't to your taste". I find that odd as I am sure you would have seen videos and images of the hotel before you arrived so not sure why that was such a surprise. We love the modern feel of the hotel and less wood in a country where there are plenty of termites is a sound long term strategy.

When you were on the houseboat you said there was a problem with the aircon and fan not working. As was explained to you at the beginning of your stay onboard only some plugs in the cabin have power all night and thats where the fan should stay plugged into. 

I could go on but I think it is fair to say that we have obviously failed to meet your expectations on many levels and for that I am sorry. We strive to try and make everyone's safari experience the best we can and obviously in this instance we have, in your opinion, fallen short.

I wish it was different but I guess we will have to live with a two star review in perpetuity and hope that people see it for what it is! 

Toby

Some VIP wanted to use the bunker so we were expelled. We did not appreciate being treated like second class tourist.
Have been in contact with Toby of Pangolin I enumerated in much greater detail  the many more negative instances we had on safari. Hopefully they will be corrected. In the mean time the ruined photo opportunities still leave a bitter taste.

title: So close to brilliant.
text:
I joined one of the scheduled Pangolin Photo Tours to Chobe and Botswana. It was a very mixed experience. The locations chosen for the tour provided a great mix of photo opportunities from Elephant families at the Chobe River, to being surrounded by a lion pride in the Kalahari. The big picture was really well organized and it should have been a brilliant tour. Unfortunately, it was in the details of the tour things were not looked after. Our photo guide was relatively inexperienced and missed several opportunities to enhance our tour and on one instance literally make an unforgivable error.  For me the most serious issues were due to the combination of the local driver/guides from the Botswana lodges and our photo host. The worst was one morning when guests in one of the two vehicles were set up for a literal "shot of a lifetime" when the 2nd vehicle (with our photo host on board) literally drove into shot not once but twice! The first time they disturbed the resting lioness who was going to be the subject of the photograph causing her to move on and our vehicle to have to reposition to try to line up the sunrise shot again. Only to have it ruined for the second time.  It is highly unlikely I will ever have the opportunity to take that shot again. 

I would like to recommend Pangolin Tours, however, if you are an experienced photographer I would suggest a chat with the organizers ahead of time to ensure you have the best experience.

ownerResponse.text:
Hi Alison,

Thank you very much for your feedback on your trip with us and I would like to apologise that it did not live up to expectations.

We would welcome the opportunity to take you on a photo safari again in the hope that we can make up for this missed shot with plenty of others.

You know where we are!

All the best

Toby

text: I can't say enough about Pangolin. You get your money's worth and more here.

text:

Highly recommend this for photographic enthusiasts as well as everyone who enjoys the excitement of the Chobe wild life.

title: No place is perfect, until you go here
text: When I had an issue with a safari in the Okavango Delta, the company I had booked through pivoted and made arrangements for me to stay here for two nights. I have but one complaint, it was ONLY two nights. There cannot be anything said about this place that is not incredible. First, the staff is friendly from the pick up, to the in house staff to the team that works to make sure that your time here is as if you are family that you have known a lifetime. Because I was not originally planning on being here, I went in blind. Well, I can tell you that if you are thinking for a second of going here you have already thought too long. Just book it! I have already spent time looking at my next trip and it will be 100% with Pangolin. The boats are amazing, the team made you look better, spent time teaching me editing tips, and just cannot say enough about this company, the people and oh, the food!! The food each night was amazing.

ownerResponse.text: Hi Rick - thanks for writing such kind words, we really enjoyed having you stay with albeit very last minute, sounds like we will be seeing you again soon, can't wait!

title: How wrong was I, Totally! As it turned out.
text:
This was a holiday that my husband wanted to do. I was very sceptical about doing it. After all how much were you going to see from a boat anyway. I was afraid of using my camera, so thought this may help.
It was awesome, we had 3 days on the Houseboat and 3 days in their Lodge. Whilst in the boat we got to see 2 Lions bring down an Impala, only meters away from our boat.
Jarco & Ray, our guides, were awesome. The people we met were lovely and we got some great photos. This is a holiday that we have and will continue to recommend. It was totally worth it.

title: Photography instruction to go from zero to hero in a few short days!  With plenty of tips for the more experienced of us
text:
I'll make this short, there are no superlatives good enough to describe our time with Pangolin.

We booked the Best of Chobe safari as my wife's first foray from North America.   I had been watching all of the phenomenal YouTube content put out by Pangolin and knew they were the company to go with.  They met and exceeded every expectation I had, and I had a lot.

Our guides, Ray and Charlies, were excellent, knowing the game well and how best to position our boat or game drive vehicle for the best photos.

Walter served as our photo host.  Not only is he an excellent photographer, but he's an outstanding teacher.  I do a lot of wildlife photography, and my wife is/was a complete novice at digital photography.  By the end of the trip she was turning out outstanding images, and I learned more than a few tips.  Well worth it for both of us.

Typically, you'll do two game drives per day, either by custom-designed photography boats or game vehicles.  We had four meals daily, and the evening meals were some of the best I have ever had.  Also, while on the game drives, there are snacks, water, coffee, or sodas.  You won't go hungry!

While we both had gear (Nikon for my wife. I carry Canon R3 and Canon R5 ) they do have a loan department from which they will provide (free of charge) Canon 80Ds and 150-600mm lenses.   

The photo hosts are well-versed in the major camera manufacturers and models.

Again, I can't begin to praise Pangolin, Toby, and Guts for the incredible customer service and photo safari.

ownerResponse.text: How's wonderful to hear such great feedback, I will pass on your regards to Walter and everyone else mentioned, Im sure you came away with some fantastic images!

title: Exciting and Memorable Photo Safaris with Pangolin
text:
My wife & me and a delightful & memorable experience with the Pangolin Masai Mara Photographic Safari. Our interactions with Pangolin started almost a year earlier - Pangolin photo safaris are very popular with Wildlife Photographers and are in great demand, so one has to start early.

Sam, our Tour Planner, was patient with us and sorted all our issues in time and with understanding. we were travelling from India and had many issues to be taken care of. Thanks Sam.

Pangolin arranged our flight tickets to and stay at Samburu Saruni for our unescorted trip to Samburu. Very nice experience there. Our stay at Tamarind Tree on three occasions, airport pick-up and drop were meticulously arranged for. We thank Nira & Andy for excellent ground handling at Nairobi.

The Photo Safaris at Masai Mara was superlative. We stayed at the Mara Bush Camp Luxury Lodge inside the Masai Mara National Park. The Tents were alongside a small river, with about 50 Hippos lazing and grunting all through the day and night. 

The Camp Tents were large luxurious, with all amenities, large beds, working table and fully equipped bath/ toilet area. The food was professionally cooked and served by trained Chefs and catered to various food requirements. 

The Photo Safaris were undoubtedly the highlight of the days in Mara. Each large Pangolin Safari Jeep would have just 4 guests with competent Guide Driver and the very capable and eager to help Pangolin Photoguides. Charl, Sabine, Nelis and Andy were all professional & highly-rated photographers, who accompanied us in turns. I learnt a great deal about Wildlife photography and camera settings. Manual with Auto ISO and Eye-focus for birds were game changing for me. Thank you Charl, Sabine, Nelis & Andy.

We were lucky to see the Migration in full swing from the Jeep and also from the Hot-air Balloon. We also saw the Mara river crossing by the Wildebeests and Zebras, apart from the big cats, big five, most other animals - many with cubs and also birds. I have taken thousands of photographs, which will keep reminding us of the Kenyan Wildlife and the utterly capable Pangolin team.

Thank you Pangolin team for giving us the most exciting and wonderful wildlife experience so far.  Hopefully we meet again!

ownerResponse.text: How wonderful that you had such an amazing time on our Migration trip to the Masai Mara, it truly is a stunning location to photograph. Thanks for taking the time to write this review, lets hope you are able to join one of our other trips or join us here in Botswana!

The Camp Tents were large luxurious, with all amenities, large beds, working table and fully equipped bath/ toilet area. The food was professionally cooked and served by trained Chefs and catered to various food requirements. 

The Photo Safaris were undoubtedly the highlight of the days in Mara. Each large Pangolin Safari Jeep would have just 4 guests with competent Guide Driver and the very capable and eager to help Pangolin Photoguides. Charl, Sabine, Nelis and Andy were all professional & highly-rated photographers, who accompanied us in turns. I learnt a great deal about Wildlife photography and camera settings. Manual with Auto ISO and Eye-focus for birds were game changing for me. Thank you Charl, Sabine, Nelis & Andy.

We were lucky to see the Migration in full swing from the Jeep and also from the Hot-air Balloon. We also saw the Mara river crossing by the Wildebeests and Zebras, apart from the big cats, big five, most other animals - many with cubs and also birds. I have taken thousands of photographs, which will keep reminding us of the Kenyan Wildlife and the utterly capable Pangolin team.

Thank you Pangolin team for giving us the most exciting and wonderful wildlife experience so far.  Hopefully we meet again!

title: Perfect photography trip in Masai Mara
text:
This was our second trip, this time to the Masai Mara to see the Great migration. we stayed at the Mara Bush camp and it was a wonderful experience. Not only did we see the great 5 and migration but many more beautiful moments shared with fellow guests. our group of 16 had 4 cars with a driver and the Pangolin photo guides - happy to see Sabine and Charl again! Every one of them an expert himself with certain special interests in wildlife changed the cars daily to give as an even more profound and interesting look at nature and photography. The accommodation is wonderful, directly on the river with lots of hippos and at night a Masai with a spear brings you back to your (luxury) tent. The camp is fairly new therefore the rooms are Wonderfull with a large deck overlooking the river. Everything was very well planned and executed by Pangolin Safaris. The food in the camp and on the trips were delicious and in fact incredible for being out in the bush. Lunches were served underneath beautiful old trees with wildlife surrounding us. 
Leaving in the morning at 6 am and returning at 7 pm is intense but if you are into photography and wildlife this gives you the best chance to see everything. It is on some days possible to stay in the camp half days but not on all. The highlight was the Balloon trip with lunch afterwards - Sergei: best pilot ever! 
...and there is always help with post production too. the pictures added are I-phone only. 
Tipping is extra for the drivers and Lodge stuff - but can be done with CC.
Thanks to our guides Sabine & Charl, Nellis and Andy!

ownerResponse.text: Hi Barbara - wonderful to hear that you enjoyed our Masai Mara trip, Charl & Sabine will be absolutely delighted that you had such a great time! Hope to see you in Chobe soon!

title: Best photographic tour in Botswana
text: A truly bespoke photographic experience. My wife and I took a seven-day tour both on the Chobe River and Chobe national park. This tour could not have been improved in any conceivable way. Perfection in all aspects from start to finish. For anyone interested in photography in Africa whether an expert or a novice photographer this is a good as it gets. Sabine was the consummate photo guide. Professional, warm, generous, and highly skilled as a photographer and most importantly as an educator both in photography and as a naturalist. She was incredibly helpful to my wife who was a novice photographer but even with some personal photographic experience I learned a lot from her, and I got the best pictures of my life. Her knowledge of the animals was also amazing. The motel and boat staff were the most friendly and caring people I've ever met anywhere in the world. The accommodations both on the houseboat and later in the motel were very comfortable and the food and drinks were excellent and abundant. The photographic boat was certainly the best on the river. Where other tours had their guests sitting on lawn furniture, we had 360 degree swiveling chairs with an Arca Swiss gimbal to mount our cameras and with only 8 single row seats on the boat there was never anyone blocking your view. On land in Chobe national park, we had open safari vehicles with no windows to block our view or cameras. They even had coffee and insulated parkas to warm us up both on the boats and safari trucks. One of the things that Pangolin did particularly well was match our interests with other guests who were equally interested in photography. As far as photo tips I took most of my pictures on a full frame camera with a 100-500 zoom. However, for some bird photography and long-distance shots I was happy to have a 600mm lens with a 1.4 teleconverter (nice but not necessary). If something exciting was happening such as lions at a kill, we were able to extend our stay rather than rush back to get an early lunch or evening sundowner which was still waiting for us when we got there. Although what you see may vary slightly depending on the time of year, we saw an unimaginable amount and variety of wildlife (Cape buffalo, baboons, giraffes, zebras, wild dogs, crocodiles, hippos, all kinds of antelope, lions feeding, leopards, vast number of elephants with babies, and some of the most colorful birds I have ever seen). Thank you, Pangolin for making this trip the greatest of my life.  Rob

ownerResponse.text: Thanks Rob - it's wonderful to hear that you had such a fantastic trip (was there anything you didn't see?), plus its sounds like you got some great shots also! thanks agin for the review!

title: Our first photo safari and we loved it!
text:
We spent 2 nights at Pangolin Photo safaris at Kasane and couldn’t be more happy that we took the decision to come here! We are not serious photographers and so were a little apprehensive before coming that we may feel out of place amongst other seasoned photographers we expected to meet. Our fears were allayed from the moment we arrived- everyone made us feel welcome and assured us we would be fine. Our photography host was Janine and she was brilliant. Over our 4 sessions (3 on the boat and one land safari), we were given a practical cash course on photography 101 (including one session on theory!). Janine was a big reason why we enjoyed our stay as she was so friendly and willing to answer our every question. We appreciated her clear, simple explanations of different camera settings, rationale for each etc. Even our 12 year old daughter enjoyed the experience and could follow along. 

The design of the boats is excellent and played a big role in providing us with a great experience. The boat seats can swivel 360 degrees so we can quickly turn to wherever the animal(s) or bird(s) happens to be. The cameras are mounted on a strong steel frame which can also swivel completely to point to any direction. With Janine’s regular and clear instructions on what camera settings to have for different shots, it made it so easy for novices like us. And it  also allowed us the freedom to enjoy the wind on our faces and the beautiful landscape!

Viewing wildlife from a boat compared to the typical land safaris has its own special charm. By the time we reached Pangolin in Kasane we had already spent 8 days in Botswana safari lodges (15 land safaris in total) so had already seen a lot of wildlife, but seeing the animals and birds from a boat in the Chobe river gave us an altogether different experience. For example, watching the big herd of thirsty elephants bound into the river or getting up close to the birds on our boat without scaring them away. 

We particularly enjoyed our two morning photo safaris along the Chobe River. We saw abundant wildlife (we were particularly lucky to see 8 lions, in addition to lots of elephants, buffaloes, impala and numerous birds (including getting great photos of the white-fronted bee-eater, malachite kingfisher, little bee-eater etc).

The rooms are clean and functional. Food was really good both in terms of spread and flavor. Chef Robson accommodated the different dietary requirements of our family and each meal was filling and satisfying.
 
In short if you are interested in wildlife/safaris and want to hone your photography skills, there is no better way to learn than join a photo safari experience like Pangolin, set amidst the natural beauty of the Chobe River with abundant wildlife and beautiful birds!

We will now practice what we learnt and are already looking forward to another photo safari with Pangolin!

ownerResponse.text: Wow!! Such a wonderful read, thanks so much for taking the time to write such a great review! Janine will be delighted that she was able to get you some amazing shots and give you a wonderful experience! Thanks very much!

title: Amazing trip
text:
We organized our trip to Botswana through Pangolin.  Pangolin arranged all the camps, all the flight connections within Botswana, and arranged for the guides.  The trip was exceptional - all the recommended camps were excellent, every connection worked, the guides were amazing.  And, of course, the entire trip was geared towards photography.  We had a wonderful time and returned home with amazing photos and amazing memories.

It is possible to go on a set trip with Pangolin in which case you would have a photo guide along the entire time.  However, they will also book a custom trip and accommodate specific requests/itineraries. For the latter, you might not always have a photo guide but the wildlife guides were all incredibly knowledgeable and well versed in how to position the vehicles for photographers. All in all, well organized, amazing guides, very accommodating, spectacular place.

ownerResponse.text: Thanks very much for taking the time to write such a Fantastic review! It's our pleasure to make sure you enjoy all aspects of your trip, whether it's with one off our direct properties or one of our sister camps! Hope to see you soon!

title: Come River, Delta and Kalahari safari
text: We are recently returned from an excellent Pangolin Photographic safari. Firstly we stayed on the house boat on the Chobe  River with the stunning sunrises and sunsets, crocs, hypos and Ellies. Next it was Pom Pom camp in the Delta with so many leopards and a pair of black mambas doing a courtship dance. This camp was an unexpected stay due to technical difficulties at the planned camp but was a wonderful reserve with fantastic staff. Finally we were at Dinaka in the Kalahari with the beautiful black maned lions. Our experience was completely enhanced by having Danielle as our photographic guide. She was a great in every respect but particularly in juggling the diverse needs of the 8 of us in the group with very differing levels of photographic experience. A big thank you to Danielle for encouraging me as a complete novice and making sure our photos on future safaris will be of a much higher standard!

ownerResponse.text:
Hi Jackie,

Thank you so much for your glowing review….will make sure that Danielle sees it. Also delighted that PomPom lived up to its reputation for you…courting mambas! Wow!

We hope you will join us again some day for more adventures. Until then.

A big thank you to Danielle for encouraging me as a complete novice and making sure our photos on future safaris will be of a much higher standard!
translated_title: Come River, Delta and Kalahari safari
translated_text: We are recently returned from an excellent Pangolin Photographic safari. Firstly we stayed on the house boat on the Chobe  River with the stunning sunrises and sunsets, crocs, hypos and Ellies. Next it was Pom Pom camp in the Delta with so many leopards and a pair of black mambas doing a courtship dance. This camp was an unexpected stay due to technical difficulties at the planned camp but was a wonderful reserve with fantastic staff. Finally we were at Dinaka in the Kalahari with the beautiful black maned lions. Our experience was completely enhanced by having Danielle as our photographic guide. She was a great in every respect but particularly in juggling the diverse needs of the 8 of us in the group with very differing levels of photographic experience. A big thank you to Danielle for encouraging me as a complete novice and making sure our photos on future safaris will be of a much higher standard!

text: When I found Pangolin, I cancelled a reservation I had at another company and let Pangolin plan our Botswana adventure.  I'm so glad I did!  Four days at Pangolin advanced my photographic skills dramatically.  Going out each day, twice a day, on the boat with a photo guide giving constant advice and feedback was intense and just want I wanted and needed.  My husband was not a photographer, but was outfitted with a camera and zoom lens and had fun learning a bit about photography.  The hotel is nice, the food excellent, and the company superb.  There's a great vibe as those from around the world are brought together by a shared interest.  The staff could not have been more accommodating.  This is a great value.  If you have an interest in photography and want to advance your skills shooting wildlife, go!  You'll be so glad you did.

ownerResponse.text:

All the best

Team Pangolin.

title: Great photo safari
text:
We spent 3 nights on the house boat and 3 nights at the Pangolin Hotel. If you’re an avid wildlife or bird photographer it is an unforgettable way to experience the Chobe River and Chobe National Park. 
Our photo guide for the trip was William Steel who has an amazing knowledge of the bird life along the river and was extremely helpful for setting up your camera to get the best of every photo opportunity. 
The live presented endless opportunities to photograph hippos and elephants too.

title: Fabulous time
text:
We stayed for 5 nights recently at the hotel. This is our 2nd stay with Pangolin as the first time in August 2018 was just a half day event.

If you planning your first safari and not into photography yet, may I suggest you make Pangolin your first stop n stay. As others have mentioned this a photographer’s dream as the setup is catered to the needs of a photographer even like a novice like me. 

The Chobe river is one of the most accessible place in Botswana for wildlife sightings and in abundance. This makes sightings easy and photo opportunities plentiful. Just going on a safari with your iPhone or pocket camera, does not do justice to the trip. The best part of Pangolin, everything is taken care of and you just bring home the memories in a SD card.

The room setup is clean, comfortable with lots of storage space. Food and drinks are always available. There is a photo editing room and the photo host (Zac) are there to help you with your editing in between the activities. 

The team (Guts, Dan, Zac, Matt, Doc, Chef, Walter, Shorty) are full of fun. We had 3 different photo hosts (Guts, Zac, Walter) over the 6 days and you can tell the superior technical skills n quality of teaching of the photo hosts. One feedback for further improvement, the photo host should not be focus on taking photos on his own camera (only for a minimal), your guests are the priority and so what if the photo host miss the shot of a lifetime. Too bad I guess.

Would definitely revisit the place and encourage you to make a trip and get Guts (the master) or Zac (upcoming) to be your host.

ownerResponse.text: Thanks Michael! Great feedback and really glad you had such a great time with us, Ive passed on your words to all mentioned and lets hope we see you again soon!

text: If you love photography or just want to learn, this is the place to go. The entire team at Pangolin was super friendly and helpful. You will be the envy of all the others on the river with the setup on these boats and the others were all taking pictures of us. This experience has made me even more excited about Photography and I can't wait until I can plan my next trip with Pangolin.

title: A great photographic experience!
text: We have just returned from 9 nights at Pangolin Photo safaris with Ann & Steve Toon and a group of 8 of us altogether. .  We spent 4 nights on the houseboat which was wonderful.  It was so peaceful.  Lots of activity on the river with elephants crossing one morning right next to the houseboat.  We were taken out on the photo boats which are terrific, flat bottomed and very steady with seats down the middle and gimbals for your camera.  We spent 5 nights at the Pangolin Chobe Hotel which we really liked.  It has a modern relaxed feel and we were made to feel very welcome.  Most of the time we were out on the boats but we did have 2 game drives in Chobe National Park as well.  The hotel is great for photographers.  Lots of charging points, an editing room and lots of space in the rooms for equipment.  The food was delicious and plenty of it!  It really is a great place to stay.  Very friendly!

ownerResponse.text: Thanks fort taking the time to write such a fab review, its great that you managed to experience both the houseboat and the hotel! Ann & Steve always bring us great guests!

title: A Fabulous Time with Pangolin Photo Safaris
text: From photographing wildlife to the food to comfortable accommodations to organization, Pangolin Photo Safaris knows how to do it right. I went with 2 friends and we all had a wonderful time.  I'm not sure if this is the right safari for someone with absolutely no interest  in photography, because every boat or land game drive is designed to maximize photo opportunities. Plus one of the key features of each drive is that you are accompanied by a photo host who provides exceptional guidance on improving your photography skills. We were very, very fortunate to have Sabine as our photo host.  She was superb at making everyone on the trip, from beginner photographers to more advanced photographers, feel absolutely comfortable asking questions, seeking advice, and increasing their photography skills. The guides are also top notch.  How they see  the tiniest of wildlife while driving a land cruiser or a boat is amazing.  They help spot the wildlife and then work hard to maneuver the vehicle in a position where everyone gets a good photo shot. I've been on other safaris where the tour guide is more interested in getting  his/her own photos than helping the clients. This is definitely not that type of tour!  Sabine spent 100% of her time helping us get the shots that we wanted.  She also spent time in the afternoons providing one-on-one instruction on photo editing/reviewing our photos. These were also invaluable sessions. All in all, if you want a unique photography experience that leaves you completely satisfied at the end, you will not go wrong with choosing to go with Pangolin.

ownerResponse.text: Thanks so much - you are absolute correct in that this is a safari for all levels of wildlife photography enthusiasts! Its great to hear that Sabine was able to help you improve your photography and im sure the results were some cracking images! Thanks for the fab review!

original_title: A Fabulous Time with Pangolin Photo Safaris
original_text: From photographing wildlife to the food to comfortable accommodations to organization, Pangolin Photo Safaris knows how to do it right. I went with 2 friends and we all had a wonderful time.  I'm not sure if this is the right safari for someone with absolutely no interest  in photography, because every boat or land game drive is designed to maximize photo opportunities. Plus one of the key features of each drive is that you are accompanied by a photo host who provides exceptional guidance on improving your photography skills. We were very, very fortunate to have Sabine as our photo host.  She was superb at making everyone on the trip, from beginner photographers to more advanced photographers, feel absolutely comfortable asking questions, seeking advice, and increasing their photography skills. The guides are also top notch.  How they see  the tiniest of wildlife while driving a land cruiser or a boat is amazing.  They help spot the wildlife and then work hard to maneuver the vehicle in a position where everyone gets a good photo shot. I've been on other safaris where the tour guide is more interested in getting  his/her own photos than helping the clients. This is definitely not that type of tour!  Sabine spent 100% of her time helping us get the shots that we wanted.  She also spent time in the afternoons providing one-on-one instruction on photo editing/reviewing our photos. These were also invaluable sessions. All in all, if you want a unique photography experience that leaves you completely satisfied at the end, you will not go wrong with choosing to go with Pangolin.

title: Our Pangolin Experience was Exceptional
text:
We picked Pangolin for our first trip to Africa, as we're avid photographers and had become enchanted by the Chobe wildlife and the Pangolin crew and hotel, watching their adventures during the pandemic from our home in the US. We opted for the "Best of the Chobe" package, which split our seven-day trip between the Pangolin Voyager House Boat and the Pangolin Hotel. 

This trip was absolutely spectacular--well organized, well run, and extremely hospitable and accommodating to everyone who is visiting. The entire staff made us feel welcomed, appreciated, and the rapport within our group and the others staying at the hotel was wonderful. 

We had the unfortunate experience of losing our baggage on the way to Botswana, and we got daily updates from Dan and Ndibo, who were calling the airline each day on our behalf, offering to bring items and our bag to us on the houseboat, if it arrived. The whole team went out of their way to ensure we had everything we needed, tracking down toothbrushes, hairbrushes, and even a photo card reader for us to improve our stay. 

Our group was mixed in terms of photographic background and skill, and our photo host Will  was excellent and ensuring everyone understood how their camera worked, without slowing things down for experienced photographers. Our boat and game drive guides also were incredible in their knowledge and ability to help us get the 'perfect' positioning for our shots went a long way--even going out of their way to ensure that I got multiple attempts at the perfect shot of a malachite kingfisher, as we hadn't seen many. 

The food and service were also exceptional and we're already discussing when we'd like to visit again.

This was one of the first trips I ever felt emotional about leaving the place, people, and experience behind--it's just that perfect. If you're on the fence, just book it! You won't regret it.

ownerResponse.text: Thank you so much for taking the time to write such a beautiful review, ourselves we are just chuffed that you enjoyed your stay with pangolin both on the houseboat and the at the hotel! lets hope we see you again soon!

title: Absolutely fantastic: people, location, food and photography !
text:
As an introduction to Africa and to safaris in particular, Pangolin Photo Safaris could not have done anything better to enhance our experience. From the quality and professionalism of the expert photographers’ tuition and the guides abundant knowledge of the wildlife of the Chobe area to the very high standards of the accommodation and cuisine in particular. Guests were welcomed back home to the houseboat or lodge hotel by warm, enthusiastic staff who clearly enjoyed working for Pangolin.
The expert photographers were able to engage with all guests regardless of their prior photographic experiences or ability. In our case I was able to demonstrably improve my skill set from being a competent landscape photographer to now being able to capture both the essence and detail of subject matter that actually moves and has a mind of its own. Over the course of the holiday my wife went from being a strictly ‘point, shoot and not expect much’ to being able to take photographs that she was happy, on occasions, to share with others. I thoroughly enjoyed seeing her confidence grow and for her to actually begin to enjoy a hobby that I have long appreciated. Pangolin Photo Safaris either provide you with an excellent camera and lens or the experts give guidance on using your own equipment.
The level of service by the staff and the commitment to customer satisfaction is evident from the outset. The photo hosts join their small group (no more than eight) for sundowners and dinner and seem to take a genuine interest in ensuring that their guests get the best experience possible. My wife also enjoyed one afternoon just soaking up the environment, with a pair of binoculars, without concentrating on the photography. That was fine too.
Ours was a truly memorable holiday. We left with a strong desire to return and to do go on another Photo Safari with Pangolin.

ownerResponse.text: Thanks Roy such magic words! Its great that you were able to have such a great experience and im certain you managed to get some great shots during your stay! Till the next time.....

title: Pangolin is the best
text: My family and I used Pangolin Photo Safaris for our recent travels to Botswana. We found the  staff and professional photographer guides to be exceptional. Not only were the arrangements  at the safari camps suburb, the staff at each location were helpful and friendly with attention to our every need. The professional photo guides were present at our Chobe River location and Okavango Delta locations. As an avid photographer I found the photo guides superb. They helped me expand my photography skills and put me in the right place at the right time.  I was traveling with three beginner photographers who learned a great deal on how to use the DSLRs that Pangolin provided. The photo boats that Pangolin has on the Chobe River were exceptional with comfortable seats, covering for shade and gimbal type camera supports that make hours of photography fun and comfortable. The best boats on the river. Our arrangements on the Okavango Delta and the Kalahari Desert (Danaka Camp) were superb with phenomenal wildlife opportunities, excellent guides, wonderful food and everything you need to create an experience of a lifetime. I would highly recommend Pangolin Photo Safaris for anyone going to Botswana. They are superb. As an avid photographer I will use them for further travels to Africa.

ownerResponse.text: Hi David - its fabulous to hear how much you enjoyed your entire trip with us, it really is an amazing itinerary including Chobe, Delta & Kalahari. You are right just a lot of diversity in the species you will see, thanks for review and great to hear you will be back with us again!

title: Photographers Heaven
text: My partner and I did the Best of Chobe Safari. We spent 3 days on the House boat the Pangolin Voyager and 3 nights in the Pangolin Chobe Hotel. The warm welcome by the staff in both places were exceptional, no request was too hard to accomodate, including supplying us with a new tube of toothpaste. The chefs, well they make deliciousness out of the ingredients they have on hand, you certainly did not go hungry. Our photographic host Danielle was FANTASTIC. She ensured every guest had one on one time with her, explaining various aspects of photography as well as light room. Due to her excellent teaching skills I have a new passion capturing birds in flight, something I never believed I would be able to do. I still need loads of practice but this was a great starting point. Danielle pointed out alternate concepts like high and low key when the conditions were right, WOW what great images these concepts produced. I simply can't recommend the whole experience enough. We met as strangers and left as friends. I hope I will be able to return in the not too distant future.

ownerResponse.text: Thanks so much for taking the time to write such a beautiful review, everyone at Pangolin is delighted you enjoyed your stay and its great that you got to learn new techniques and have developed a new passion for birds in flight!
